methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Standard Subsea is a seed-stage company in the Ocean & Maritime sector founded by Christopher Amidon, having raised $10M+. The comparable universe consists of three private peers (Clippership, NetworkOcean, Thalassa Robotics) with no public market comparables available in this emerging subsector.

The valuation analysis relies on a single applicable method: stage-progression analysis. Using the $10M total capital raised as a base value proxy, application of a typical 2.5x multiplier for advancement from seed to the next funding stage yields an implied valuation of $25M. This represents a standard step-up assumption rather than company-specific operating metrics or revenue multiples, reflecting the early-stage and pre-revenue nature of the business.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $21M to $29M, with a median point estimate of $25M. The narrow band reflects reliance on a single methodology. Key uncertainties include the absence of public market benchmarks for the Ocean & Maritime technology sector, limited visibility into the three private comparables' financials and stage progression, and dependence on whether the $10M raised figure appropriately reflects equity value versus option pool dilution. Additional clarity on product development milestones, customer traction, and technical differentiation would materially refine this assessment.
